title: Malvastrum pygmaeum
taxonomy: Malvastrum pygmaeum is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the mallow or hibiscus family. It belongs to the genus Malvastrum, which is a group of plants native to tropical regions of the Americas. Malvastrum pygmaeum is typically classified as a shrub or a small tree, characterized by its small leaves and vibrant flowers. The plant is often found in dry, scrubby areas and is an important part of the ecosystem in its native habitats. Through its classification, Malvastrum pygmaeum is recognized as a distinct species within the broader family of Malvaceae.
